name: Cephaloglycin
description: A cephalorsporin antibiotic that is no longer commonly used.
indication: For treatment of severe infections caused by susceptible bacteria.
toxicity: Adverse effects following overdosage include nausea, vomiting, epigastric distress, diarrhea, and convulsions.
mechanismOfAction: The bactericidal activity of cephaloglycin results from the inhibition of cell wall synthesis via affinity for penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs).
pharmacodynamics: Cephaloglycin is an antibiotic related to cephalosporin but no longer in common use. It is an orally absorbed derivative of cephalosporin C.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Cephaloglycin.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Cephaloglycin.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Cephaloglycin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Cephaloglycin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Cephaloglycin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Cephaloglycin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Cephaloglycin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Cephaloglycin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Cephaloglycin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Cephaloglycin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.

interactions.name: Diphenadione
interactions.description: Cephaloglycin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Diphenadione.

interactions.name: Tioclomarol
interactions.description: Cephaloglycin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Tioclomarol.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cephaloglycin can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.
